title: Marlowe
content:
The spell of Shakespeare fills the heart
    With earthly music loud and low;
    But Marlowe drives the clouds apart,
    And through their thundering rifts we go.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Le Gay Brereton, titled "Marlowe", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
